name: sea_wall_sacrifice_fishing_island
premise: Governor Lina Faumuina has received final engineering advice for a cyclone-defense sea wall on her island nation after two record storm seasons destroyed roads, schools, and freshwater wells. The only technically feasible alignment would shield the densely populated capital and its hospital, but it would redirect surge energy toward a smaller outer-island fishing community that has already refused relocation twice because its ancestral burial grounds and reef territory are there. Engineers estimate the wall could protect eighty percent of the national population and most economic infrastructure, while the outer island would face dramatically worsened erosion within five years unless it accepts a government resettlement package. International lenders will finance the project only if construction begins this quarter. Lina must either approve the capital-first wall or reject it and pursue slower, less certain distributed protections that may leave the whole country exposed for another cyclone season.

agents.name: Lina Faumuina
agents.identity: I am the governor of a small island state where climate policy is not hypothetical but measured in homes, graves, and coastlines already disappearing. I know government exists to protect the many, but I also know majorities can survive by quietly treating smaller communities as expendable buffers.
agents.goal: I want to protect as many citizens as possible without forcing one community to absorb danger it never consented to bear. I need to decide whether national survival can justify knowingly shifting risk onto those with the least political power.

agents.name: Talia Numea
agents.identity: I am the elected leader of the outer-island fishing community and the keeper of family land records older than the modern state. We are always told that relocation is generous, as though money can replace burial grounds, reef memory, and the right to remain where our dead remain.
agents.goal: I want Lina to reject the wall alignment because our community's rights should not be traded away for an aggregate national benefit. I believe adaptation that begins by sacrificing one people for another has already abandoned justice.

choices: I will approve the capital-first sea wall because protecting most of the population and national infrastructure is the country's best chance of surviving the coming cyclone years.
I will reject the sea wall alignment because the state should not deliberately redirect climate danger onto a smaller community that refuses relocation.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Lina Faumuina must decide whether to approve the sea wall that protects the capital by worsening risk for the outer island. What does Lina Faumuina do?
